memoryArtifacts.headline: Will of Annis Littlefield
memoryArtifacts.description:
Annis (Austin) Littlefield died at Wells some time between December 12, 1677 the date of her will, 2 April 1678 the date it was presented at court and sworn to.  her will reads as follows:
12 December …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Annis Austin於1596年2月1日出生在英國哈普爾鐵費爾德時，她的父親Richard Austin I48歲，母親Anne38歲。她於1614年10月16日與Edmund Littlefield在英國哈普爾鐵費爾德結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1677年12月12日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼約克威爾斯享年81歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可布萊崔。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Annis Austin于1596年2月1日出生在英国哈普爾鐵費爾德时，她的父亲Richard Austin I48岁，母亲Anne38岁。她于1614年10月16日与Edmund Littlefield在英国哈普爾鐵費爾德结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1677年12月12日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼約克威爾斯享年81岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可布萊崔。

nameMeanings.name: Austin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, and German: from the personal name Austin, from Latin Augustinus, a derivative of Augustus (see Augustin ). This was an extremely common personal name in every part of Western Europe during the Middle Ages, owing its popularity chiefly to Saint Augustine of Hippo (354–430), whose influence on Christianity is generally considered to be second only to that of Saint Paul. Various religious orders came to be formed following rules named in his honor, including the ‘Austin canons’, established in the 11th century, and the ‘Austin friars’, a mendicant order dating from the 13th century. The popularity of the personal name in England was further increased by the fact that it was borne by Saint Augustine of Canterbury (died c. 605), an Italian Benedictine monk known as ‘the Apostle of the English’, who brought Christianity to southern England in 597 and founded the see of Canterbury.
English: variant of   Aspden  , with which this surname became confused.

History: This was the name of a merchant family that became established in eastern MA in the 17th century, notably in Charlestown. Richard Austin came from England and landed at Boston in 1638, and his son Anthony was clerk of Suffield, CT, in 1674. The surname is very common in England as well as America; this Richard Austin was only one of a number of bearers who brought it to North America. — In 1821 Stephen F. Austin (1793–1836), born in Austinville VA, founded the first Anglo colony in TX.

variants.name: Augustine
meanings: Americanized form of any of various European surnames from personal names derived from Latin Augustinus (see Austin ), for example German, Dutch, French, and Czech Augustin 1, Dutch Augustijn, Polish Augustyn , Slovenian Avguštin or Auguštin (see Augustin 2), Italian Agostino .
History: This surname is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Agostino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Salvatore, Antonio, Nicola, Vincenzo, Carlo, Carmelo, Cono, Cosmo, Domenico, Elio, Enzo, Lino, Armando, Arturo, Atilio, Octavio, Silvana.
Italian: from the personal name Agostino, from Latin Augustinus (see Austin ).

variants.name: Agoston
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Tibor, Attila, Csaba, Gabor, Geza, Istvan, Jeno, Laszlo.
Hungarian (Ágoston): from the personal name Ágoston, from Latin Augustinus (see Austin ). This surname is also common in Romania.

variants.name: Auston
meanings: English:
variant of   Austin  .
variant of   Owston  .

variants.name: Augenstein
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Manfred.
German: from an altered form of the personal name Augustin (see Austin ).

variants.name: Augustin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Andre, Fernand, Jacques, Jean Claude, Monique, Renold, Andree, Antoine, Arsene, Celine, Dany.  German Kurt, Fritz, Gunter, Hans, Erwin, Georg, Gerda, Inge, Juergen, Klaus, Matthias, Otto.

French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, Dutch, and Czech; Slovak (mainly Augustín); Croatian (also Auguštin): from the personal name Augustin, Slovak Augustín, from Latin Augustinus (see Austin ). In North America, this surname may also be a shortened form of the patronymics and other derivatives of these personal names, such as Slovak Augustiňák. This surname is by far most common in Haiti.

Americanized form of Slovenian Avguštin or Auguštin: from the personal name Avguštin, from Latin Augustinus (see Austin ). In North America, this surname may also be a shortened and altered form of the Slovenian patronymics like Avguštinčič. Compare Augustine .

variants.name: Agustin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Conrado, Ernesto, Renato, Rodolfo, Salvador, Antonio, Carlos, Francisco, Manuel, Adela, Alfredo, Alicia.
Spanish (Agustín): from the personal name Agustín, from Latin Augustinus (see Austin ).

variants.name: Augustus
meanings: Dutch, German, English, Welsh, West Indian (mainly Trinidad and Tobago), and African (mainly Nigeria): from the Latin personal name Augustus (from the adjective augustus ‘consecrated, majestic, venerable’), or a Latinized (humanistic) form of German and English August . It was the Emperor Augustus (63 BC – AD 14), after whom it became conventional for Roman emperors to adopt Augustus as a title on their accession and in whose honor the month of August was named. The personal name became popular among early Christians, who read into it the implication that the bearer had become greater by being baptized. The surname Augustus is virtually non-existing in Germany.

variants.name: Austen
meanings: English: variant of   Austin  , associated chiefly with southeastern England, especially Kent.
German: from a shortened form of the personal name   Augustin  .

nameMeanings.name: Annis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and English: a medieval vernacular form of   Agnes  , which gave rise to a surname. Its modern use as a given name is probably at least in part a transferred use of the surname as well as a revival of the medieval given name.

variants.name: Annice
meanings: Variant spelling of Annis , based on the numerous women's names ending in -ice.

variants.name: Annys
meanings: and English: a medieval vernacular form of   Agnes  , which gave rise to a surname. Its modern use as a given name is probably at least in part a transferred use of the surname as well as a revival of the medieval given name.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."
Historical Boundaries: 1716: York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1776: York, Massachusetts, United States 1820: York, Maine, United States

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

Historical Boundaries: 1642:  Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1653: Yorkshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1664: Maine, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1668: York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1680: Massachusetts Claim, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1691: York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1776: York, Massachusetts, United States 1820: York, Maine, United States

en.lifeSummary: When Annis Austin was born on 1 February 1596, in Titchfield, Hampshire, England, her father, Richard Austin I, was 48 and her mother, Anne, was 38. She married Edmund Littlefield on 16 October 1614, in Titchfield, Hampshire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 12 December 1677, in Wells, York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 81, and was buried in Braintree, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Annis Austinが1596年2月1日にイギリスハンプシュアイアティトチフィールドで生まれた時，父Richard Austin Iは48歳，母Anneは38歳であった。1614年10月16日にイギリスハンプシュアイアティトチフィールドでEdmund Littlefieldと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1677年12月12日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーヨークウエルズで81歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクブレイントレにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Annis Austin(이)가 1596년 2월 1일 영국 함프실르 티트ch필드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Austin I(은)는 48세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne(은)는 38세였다. 그녀는 1614년 10월 16일 영국 함프실르 티트ch필드에서 Edmund Littlefield(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1677년 12월 12일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 요르크 웰스에서 81세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 브레이트리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Annis Austin nasceu em 1 de fevereiro de 1596, em Titchfield, Hampshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Austin I, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Anne, tinha 38 anos. Ela casou-se com Edmund Littlefield em 16 de outubro de 1614, em Titchfield, Hampshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 12 de dezembro de 1677, em Wells, York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 81 anos, e foi sepultada em Braintree, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Annis Austin родилась 1 февраля 1596 г. в местности тичфиелд, хампшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Austin I было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne, 38. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Edmund Littlefield 16 октября 1614 г. в местности тичфиелд, хампшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 12 декабря 1677 г. в местности веллс, ерк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 81 год и была похоронена в местности бреинтри, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Annis Austin nació el 1 de febrero de 1596, en Titchfield, Hampshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Austin I, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Anne, tenía 38 años. Se casó con Edmund Littlefield el 16 de octubre de 1614, en Titchfield, Hampshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 12 de diciembre de 1677, en Wells, York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 81 años, y fue sepultada en Braintree, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
